title_en: Prayer in Al-Mudawwanah: Early Maliki Salah Law
title_ar: الصلاة في المدونة: فقه الصلاة المالكي المبكر
content_en:
The prayer chapters of Al-Mudawwanah record the earliest systematic statement of Maliki salah law, transmitted through Ibn al-Qasim from his direct study under Imam Malik. The questions preserved in these chapters reflect the actual concerns of early Muslim communities in Medina, Egypt, and North Africa.

On the times of prayer, Al-Mudawwanah reflects the Maliki school's distinctive positions, particularly on the preferred time for the zuhr prayer. The Maliki school recommends delaying the zuhr prayer — in warm climates — until the worst of the midday heat has passed (ibrad al-zuhr), based on the prophetic hadith recommending this practice. Ibn al-Qasim transmits Malik's consistent adherence to this practice and its recommended status in the school.

The Maliki positions on the adhan and iqamah are recorded with the details that distinguish the school. The Maliki adhan follows the Madinan practice, which includes reciting the Shahada twice in each statement (tarji') as a standard element, not a personal variant. The iqamah in the Maliki school uses each phrase once rather than doubled as in some Hanafi practices, following the specific transmission from Bilal that Malik regarded as the authentic Medinan practice.

On the witr prayer, Al-Mudawwanah records Malik's view that it is not wajib but is strongly recommended (sunnah mu'akkadah). The Maliki school's position on the witr's form — one raka'ah alone, three raka'ahs with a salam after two and then one more, or other forms — is discussed through the questions posed to Ibn al-Qasim. Malik's preference, as recorded in Al-Mudawwanah, was to follow the practice he had observed the people of Medina following.

The chapter on jumu'ah in Al-Mudawwanah records Malik's insistence that Friday prayer is the most important communal obligation of the week and must not be missed without a valid excuse. The Maliki school's requirements for the validity of jumu'ah — including the number of participants required — are recorded through Ibn al-Qasim's responses, which show some variation in Malik's transmitted positions and the tradition's effort to resolve them.

The chapters on prayer for the traveler record the Maliki positions on shortening (qasr) and combining (jam') prayers. The Maliki school is distinctive in treating the shortening of the four-raka'ah prayers during travel as obligatory — the prayer should be shortened, and offering the full four raka'ahs while traveling is considered makruh (disliked). This stricter reading of the prophetic practice is characteristic of the Maliki school's approach.
title_id: Salat dalam Al-Mudawwanah: Hukum Salah Maliki Awal
content_id:
Bab-bab salat dalam Al-Mudawwanah merekam pernyataan sistematis paling awal tentang hukum salah Maliki, yang ditransmisikan melalui Ibn al-Qasim dari studinya langsung di bawah Imam Malik. Pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang tersimpan dalam bab-bab ini mencerminkan keprihatinan nyata dari masyarakat Muslim awal dan cara Imam Malik meresponsnya dengan panduan yang praktis sekaligus berakar kuat dalam sunnah.

Posisi Maliki tentang bacaan Al-Fatihah dibahas secara rinci. Mazhab Maliki mewajibkan makmum membaca Al-Fatihah ketika tidak mendengar bacaan imam (dalam salat sirriyah), namun dalam salat jahriyah ada perbedaan pendapat internal apakah makmum perlu membaca atau cukup mendengarkan. Imam Malik sendiri tampak memiliki dua pandangan yang berbeda dalam situasi yang berbeda, yang menunjukkan fleksibilitas metodologisnya.

Ruku' dan sujud dalam pandangan Maliki memiliki beberapa kekhasan. Posisi tangan selama salat, cara meletakkan tangan ketika berdiri (iras al-yadayn atau sadl — melepas tangan di samping tubuh, bukan meletakkannya di dada sebagaimana mazhab lain) adalah salah satu ciri khas salat Maliki yang dibahas dalam Al-Mudawwanah. Praktik sadl ini merupakan amalan penduduk Madinah yang Imam Malik sangat junjung tinggi sebagai sumber hukum.

Pembahasan tentang salat Jumat, salat berjamaah, imam yang batal wudhunya di tengah salat, dan berbagai kondisi darurat dalam salat semuanya tercakup dengan teliti. Al-Mudawwanah menjadi referensi wajib bagi para ulama Maliki dalam memutuskan pertanyaan-pertanyaan salat yang praktis, terutama di komunitas-komunitas Muslim di Afrika Utara dan Afrika Barat yang secara tradisional mengikuti mazhab Maliki.
content_ar:
تستعرض المدونة الكبرى أحكام الصلاة بتفصيل يعكس المذهب المالكي المبكر قبل أن تستقر اجتهاداته النهائية على يد المتأخرين. ومن أبرز ما تُبيّنه المدونة في الصلاة: مذهب مالك في القراءة خلف الإمام في الصلاة السرية والجهرية وما قاله ابن القاسم في ذلك.

ويتّسم الفقه المالكي في الصلاة بما يُعرف بـ"عمل أهل المدينة" وهو حجة مستقلة عند مالك تُرجَّح بها بعض الأحاديث الآحادية التي تُخالف ما كان عليه أهل المدينة من عمل متواتر. وهذه الخاصية المنهجية هي من أبرز ما يُميّز الأصول الفقهية المالكية.

ومن المسائل التي تبرز في باب الصلاة في المدونة: حكم السهو في الصلاة وما يُوجب سجود السهو قبل السلام وما يُوجبه بعده. وقد ذهب مالك في رواية مشهورة إلى التفريق بين سجود السهو عن الزيادة فيكون بعد السلام وعن النقص فيكون قبله.
